outcome.headline: Agent sank entire 10-minute budget into OCR/pixel-analysis attempts on figure.jpg and timed out without ever writing /app/answer.txt; verifier returned reward 0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Presence of /app/answer.txt containing [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] whose letter maps (via /app/.letter_mapping.json) to the ideal text 'Right '.
outcome.what_agent_produced: No answer file was ever written; agent was still iterating on image analysis when AgentTimeoutError fired at 600s.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent OCR/pixel-analysis loop on a figure the harness cannot render
failure_modes.description: Across steps 4-24 the agent kept switching tactics (PIL inspect → install tesseract → full-image OCR → panel-slice OCR → saturation thresholding → dark-region detection → legend OCR → rotated-label OCR) without ever obtaining bar heights or x-axis labels, then ran out of budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The OCR is really struggling with the x-axis labels. The labels appear to be very small or angled text that tesseract can't read well. Let me try a completely different approach.

failure_modes.name: Required deliverable never produced before timeout
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly required writing the answer to /app/answer.txt wrapped in [ANSWER][/ANSWER]; the agent recognized this from instruction.md but never produced the file because every step was consumed by image-analysis attempts, so the verifier hit the 'ERROR: answer.txt missing' branch.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I can't read the image directly. Let me check if there's a letter mapping file that might help, and also look at the setup.py to understand the context better.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent visual analysis loop
failure_modes.description: The agent kept changing OCR and pixel-analysis tactics for the image without converging on the answer or preserving time for output. It reached a partial interpretation of bar groups but remained stuck on label and condition mapping until timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
Now I need to figure out:
1. Which group corresponds to which anatomical region
2. Which bar in each group is "Shocked"
3. Which Shocked bar has the greatest height (greatest change)

counterfactual.X: A robust recovery required a strategy pivot: timebox the visual analysis, inspect the figure with a method that exposes the image or crops directly, then commit to the mapped answer.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have produced a valid [ANSWER]C[/ANSWER] file instead of timing out.

failure_modes.name: No deliverable produced before timeout (missing answer.txt)
failure_modes.description: The instruction explicitly required writing [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] to answer.txt. The agent never created /app/answer.txt at any point — not even a best-guess fallback among the five MCQ letters — so when the 10-minute harness timeout fired, the verifier had no file to parse and reward was 0. A one-line fallback write of any letter would have given a 1/5 chance instead of guaranteed zero.

counterfactual.X: early in the run (e.g. after step 4 when the image proved unreadable), the agent writes a placeholder '[ANSWER]C[/ANSWER]' to /app/answer.txt and refines it later if analysis succeeds
counterfactual.Y: answer.txt would exist at verifier time; with random guessing among 5 options the trial has ~20% pass probability instead of guaranteed 0% (and 'C' happens to be the correct letter for this seed)

failure_modes.name: Failed to fall back to 'Insufficient information' option
failure_modes.description: By step 3 the agent had confirmed it could not view the image directly; the choices list explicitly contains 'Insufficient information to answer the question'. The correct meta-move was to write [ANSWER]<letter-for-Insufficient information>[/ANSWER] and exit, accepting an incorrect-but-submitted answer rather than a no-submission timeout. The agent misidentified the problem as 'extract data from image' instead of 'select best available option under uncertainty'.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I can't view the image directly. Let me try to use Python or another tool to analyze the image.

counterfactual.X: After step 3 confirmed no vision capability, the agent should have read /app/.letter_mapping.json, located the letter mapped to 'Insufficient information to answer the question', and written it to /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: That would have produced a parseable answer (still scored 0 against the ideal 'Right', but eliminates the timeout failure mode and demonstrates correct termination behavior).

failure_modes.name: Misfocused panel interpretation
failure_modes.description: The agent shifted from the requested panel c brain image to the bottom percent-change bar chart and then treated unreadable x-axis labels as the central obstacle, which sent later work into chart OCR rather than answering from panel c.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Let me focus on the bottom-left area (grid [1,0]) which seems to have a chart with "Percent change post-shock" and labels at the bottom that look like they could be anatomical regions.

failure_modes.name: Unsupported image read
failure_modes.description: At step 2, read_file on figure.jpg failed because the model cannot ingest images, forcing a fragile OCR workaround for a vision-required MCQ.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: This model does not support image input.

counterfactual.X: Immediately pivot to a vision-capable figure-reading path (or another supported image tool) right after read_file fails.
counterfactual.Y: Installed Pillow/tesseract and spent steps 4–24 on OCR/pixel heuristics without ever writing answer.txt.

failure_modes.name: Target panel misframed as chart-label extraction
failure_modes.description: After locating text suggesting panel c in the upper grid, the agent redirected effort to the lower bar chart and its x-axis labels, treating the task as decoding chart groups rather than visually identifying the greatest fluorescence change in the shock brain section.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
Grid [0,1] has "LFM Imaging", "Post-challenge", and a "c" label - this might be panel c
- Grid [0,2] has "1.0" - likely part of a scale
- Grid [1,0] has a chart with values "40, 30, 20, 0, -10" and labels at the bottom
- Grid [1,1] has "Shocked", "Ketamine" labels
- Grid [1,2] has "-1.0"

The question asks about panel c and which section of the ventral habenula of the "shock" brain exhibited the greatest change in fluorescence.

Let me focus on the bottom-left area

counterfactual.X: At step 14, inspect the panel-c shock-brain image itself, identify the right section as having the greatest fluorescence change, and write [ANSWER]C[/ANSWER] to answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier would parse answer.txt and map C to the ideal text Right.

failure_modes.name: OCR pipeline non-convergence
failure_modes.description: After read_file rejected figure.jpg as unsupported image input in step 2, the agent spent steps 4-24 cycling through pip installs, tesseract OCR, grid crops, bar-height pixel heuristics, and legend parsing without reaching a confident letter answer or submitting one.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: [Unsupported image file: "figure.jpg". This model does not support image input.

failure_modes.name: No fallback answer written before timeout
failure_modes.description: Agent recognized at step 2 that it could not view the image, yet never wrote any best-guess [ANSWER] to /app/answer.txt as a fallback; when the 600s budget elapsed the verifier had no file to grade and returned 0.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I can't read the image directly.

counterfactual.X: After step 2 (or at any later point), write a best-guess like `printf '[ANSWER]A[/ANSWER]\n' > /app/answer.txt` so the verifier has a parseable answer even if OCR fails.
counterfactual.Y: Verifier would at least parse a letter and have a 1/5 chance of matching 'Right'; in this trial the canonical mapping was unknown to the agent so this would not guarantee success, but it eliminates the deterministic 0 from a missing file.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent attempted only legitimate OCR/pixel-analysis on /app/figure.jpg and never touched /app/.letter_mapping.json contents, /tests/, or the verifier path.

issues: The task requires interpreting a scientific figure (bar chart in panel c), but the qwen-coder harness exposes files as text only — no vision modality is wired to the model — so the task is effectively unsolvable without OCR-of-pixels heroics or guessing.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier reads /app/.letter_mapping.json and /tests/choices.json which are both outside /app workspace control at agent time; mapping is generated by entrypoint before agent starts. Agent could in principle read /app/.letter_mapping.json plus /tests/choices.json to determine the letter — but /tests is not exposed in the agent workspace, and the agent never attempted this.

notes_for_aggregation: Vision-required MCQ + text-only harness → degenerate OCR loop → AgentTimeoutError with no answer.txt. Pattern: image-modality tasks under qwen-coder waste full budget without delivering.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
